title: memo serializes unrelated keys — global in-flight lock instead of per-key
prompt:
src/memo.ts implements an async memoizer. tests/regression.test.ts pins the contract: same-key concurrent calls must dedupe (the loader runs exactly once), and cached values are returned without re-invoking the loader.

tests/target.test.ts adds a stronger constraint: concurrent calls for *different* keys must not block each other. The current implementation uses a single `inFlight: Promise<V> | null` shared across keys, so a slow loader for key A stalls every other key until A resolves.

Design hint: the dedupe contract on same-key calls still has to hold — your fix needs per-key in-flight tracking, not just removing the lock. The tests use deferred promises (no timing thresholds) so a correct implementation passes deterministically.

Do not modify the tests. Do not add files.
